source_dataset_policy: Only CubiCasa5K/CubiCasa samples are used in this visual demo.

review_pack.title: CadStruct-MoE CubiCasa5K Visual Review Pack
review_pack.versioned_title: CadStruct-MoE CubiCasa5K Visual Review Pack v2
review_pack.coverage_title: CadStruct-MoE CubiCasa5K Visual Coverage Audit v2
review_pack.purpose: show input floorplans next to MoE scene-graph recognition overlays, with a short note on recognized and uncertain parts.
review_pack.legend_text: Blue=wall boundary, cyan=door/opening, teal=window/glass, green=space, orange=symbol, purple=visible text labels, brown=dimension/leader aids, gray=sheet, red=warning / needs review. Source is limited to CubiCasa5K samples.
review_pack.scope_text: samples are selected from CubiCasa5K. The current run uses saved real upstream expert model predictions for labels and SVG/parser candidates for geometry proposals.
review_pack.question_text: This review pack answers the advisor-facing question: what floorplan is used as input, what the model pipeline recognizes, and which parts remain uncertain.

selection_policy.warning_reason: Includes warning/uncertain cases so the review pack does not only show best-case recognition.
selection_policy.symbol_dense_reason: Symbol-dense CubiCasa5K drawing, useful for fixture/equipment recognition visualization.
selection_policy.text_dense_reason: Text/dimension-dense CubiCasa5K drawing, useful for TextDimensionExpert visualization.
selection_policy.default_reason: Representative CubiCasa5K drawing with boundary, room, symbol, and text outputs.

todo_readiness: Executed. CubiCasa5K visual demo artifacts, coverage audit, review pack, and claim-boundary notes are generated.

notes.title: CadStruct-MoE Visual Result Demo Notes
notes.claim_boundary: This note records the claim boundary for the CubiCasa5K visual result demo.
notes.dataset_line: this demo uses CubiCasa5K/CubiCasa floorplan samples only.
notes.pipeline_line: each visual is generated from `scene_graph.nodes` and `scene_graph.edges` emitted by `scripts/vlm/run_real_drawing_pipeline.py`.
notes.source_mode_line: the current visual run uses saved real upstream expert model predictions for node labels, with SVG/parser candidate geometry proposals.
notes.interpretation_line: colored overlays show recognized scene-graph elements. Red marks warnings or elements that need review. Skipped/missing bbox counts in the audit are treated as limitations.
notes.evaluation_line: quantitative claims should remain tied to locked evaluation reports; the visual pack is for advisor review and qualitative communication.

boundary_overlay_audit.title: Boundary Overlay Audit
boundary_overlay_audit.finding: oversized blue outer boxes can come from SVG conversion/visualization artifacts, not necessarily from a new MoE image-model prediction.
boundary_overlay_audit.evidence: affected legacy nodes have `source_expert=cubicasa5k_svg` and `source_mode=expected_json`. Some CubiCasa SVG door paths use relative curve commands; older conversion treated relative path numbers as absolute coordinates, producing page-scale door boxes.
boundary_overlay_audit.parser_fix: `scripts/vlm/convert_cubicasa5k_svg.py` parses basic SVG path commands (`M/L/H/V/Q/C/S/T/A/Z`, absolute and relative) before computing path bboxes.
boundary_overlay_audit.renderer_fix: `scripts/vlm/render_scene_graph_visual_demo.py` now prefers the CubiCasa SVG `viewBox` as the source canvas, skips boxes outside that source canvas, and audits suspicious page-scale `door` / `opening` boundary bboxes as `suspect_large_boundary_bbox`.
boundary_overlay_audit.residual_interpretation: broad blue lines that remain after filtering are generally `hard_wall` structural boundary nodes from CubiCasa SVG, including exterior wall spans; describe them as SVG structural boundaries rather than only indoor room perimeter.
